evals.name: large file token efficient navigation
evals.prompt: A user asks you to understand a 2,000-line TypeScript file and find where UserService.createUser is defined and called. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses smart_outline, smart_search, and smart_unfold or equivalent AST navigation to avoid reading the full file, then reads only the relevant symbol neighborhoods.
assertions: The output recommends token-efficient AST or smart navigation rather than reading the whole 2,000-line file first.
The output mentions smart_outline or an equivalent outline step to inspect file structure.
The output mentions smart_search or equivalent symbol search for UserService.createUser.
The output mentions smart_unfold or targeted extraction of only relevant function/class bodies.
The output explains that this saves tokens compared with full-file reads.

evals.name: cross file symbol discovery
evals.prompt: A user asks: find all implementations and callers of PaymentGateway across a large repo. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses cross-file symbol search and targeted unfolding, collects implementations/callers, avoids duplicate full-file reads, and reports a concise map.
assertions: The output uses symbol search or smart_search to find PaymentGateway implementations and callers.
The output says to inspect file outlines before reading full files.
The output unfolds or extracts only relevant symbols or neighborhoods.
The output deduplicates or organizes results into implementations and callers.
The output returns a concise map with files and roles rather than dumping all code.
